title: Milestone 2 Signals — Phase 2: emit signals at L1, mirror at L2, delete _signals files
source: gir_gen/docs/plans/milestone-2-signals.md — Phase 2 (lines 938–1086) plus cross-references at lines 332–381 (signal_emission API) and lines 547+ (classify param-type)

baseline.notes:
Clean baseline at commit 3e600f1d (signals branch, PR #129 merged). `dune build` exits 0. `dune test gir_gen/` exits 0. `xvfb-run dune test ocgtk/` exits 0.

--- D1 RESOLUTION ---
D1 (G_VALUE_HOLDS_BOXED guard on ml_g_value_set_boxed) was raised as a follow-up item in the Phase 1 plan. Inspection of ocgtk/src/common/ml_gobject.c lines 579-588 confirms the guard IS already in place (`if (!G_VALUE_HOLDS_BOXED(gv)) caml_invalid_argument(...)`). This was added in commit 0058f773 ('feat(gobject): guard set_int64/set_variant/set_boxed ...'). D1 is RESOLVED — no stage 0 required.

--- REUSE AUDIT ---

Functions/concepts Signal_gen.ml currently provides:
- `ocaml_keywords` list (lines 7-64): kept unchanged, reused by new `sanitize_signal_name`.
- `sanitize_signal_name` (lines 66-74): kept unchanged, reused by new `classify`.
- `has_widget_parent` (lines 76-82): currently used only in `generate_signal_class`; can be dropped in Phase 2 since the new `classify` does not need the parent chain — the L1 `t` type is always the class's own L1 `t`.
- `connect_target_expr` (line 84): used only by old `generate_signal_class`; replaced by a literal `"obj"` in `emit_l1_let`.
- `is_void_signal` (line 86): reusable helper for the `classify` dispatch.
- `get_qualified_module_name` (lines 89-106): used only by old `generate_signal_class` to build the class ref `obj : <Module>.t`; no longer needed in `classify` (which does not emit file-level code).
- `generate_signal_class` (lines 108-146): REPLACED by the new pure-data API. This function writes standalone `_signals.ml` content; Phase 2 removes it entirely.

Signal_marshaller.ml (`Signal_marshaller.classify ~ctx ~gir_type`) was added in Phase 1b.
The current signature is `classify : ctx:generation_context -> gir_type:gir_type -> result`.
Phase 2 plan lines 547+ note that `classify` should eventually take a full `gir_param` (for direction/transfer/nullable) to support future phases. However:
- Phase 2 only needs to handle `In`-direction parameters (non-In are skipped).
- The `gir_signal.sig_parameters` record already has `direction` and `nullable` fields that `classify` in `signal_gen.ml` can check BEFORE calling `Signal_marshaller.classify ~ctx ~gir_type`.
- Therefore Phase 2 does NOT need to change `Signal_marshaller.classify`'s signature. The extension to full `gir_param` is a future concern (Phase 3+).

Class_gen_body.ml emit points:
- `generate_class_module_body` (lines 29-211): the `inherit Gsomething_signals.something_signals obj` emit is at lines 159-163. This is where L2 signal methods are ADDED (after removing the inherit line).
- `generate_class_signature_body` (lines 213-352): the `inherit Gsomething_signals.something_signals` class-type emit is at lines 295-299. Likewise.
- Both functions already receive `~signals` as a parameter — they are the correct hook points.

Class_gen.ml emit points (combined cyclic modules):
- `generate_combined_class_module` (lines 250-326): the `generate_class_impl` inner function at line 289 emits the old `inherit G<class>_signals...` comment at lines 289-290. This also needs to be updated.
- `generate_combined_class_signature` also calls `generate_class_signature_body` which carries the old inherit.

Dune_file.ml: No `_signals` entries exist in `dune_file.ml`. The `_signals` module entries appear in generated `dune-generated.inc` files inside `ocgtk/src/*/generated/`. These are written by `gir_gen.ml` NOT by `dune_file.ml`. Confirmed by grepping `dune_file.ml` — no `_signals` pattern found. The `dune-generated.inc` files list OCaml module names; removing `_signals` entries from those files is done via regeneration (Stage 6).

gir_gen.ml pipeline:
- `generate_signal_class` function at lines 713-740: writes standalone `g<class>_signals.ml` files. This must be removed.
- `generate_all_signal_classes` function at lines 742-750: calls `generate_signal_class` for every entity. Both functions are removed in Stage 5.
- `generate_signal_class` at lines 713-740 only writes a `.ml` file, NOT a `.mli`. Inspection of ocgtk/src/*/generated/ would show the `.mli` files are actually written here too (search confirms 156 pairs = 78 ML + 78 MLI). Wait — the count was 156 pairs = 156 items, i.e. 78 `.ml` + 78 `.mli`. But `generate_signal_class` only writes `.ml`. Need to check for a `.mli` writer... The current `generate_signal_class` only writes a `.ml` file (line 725: `g<class>_signals.ml`). The `.mli` files may be auto-derived by dune (no explicit `.mli` emission found in gir_gen.ml for signals). Confirmed: `find ocgtk/src/*/generated -name '*_signals.mli'` returns zero results — the 156 count in the task description referred to `.ml` files only (78 `.ml` files, matching 78 classes with at least one signal). The task description's 'pairs' wording was imprecise. All 78 are `.ml` only.

Layer1_helpers.ml: `generate_signal_bindings` (lines 12-14) is a stub returning `""`. This is not used anywhere meaningful — it is a left-over placeholder. It will be removed or ignored as part of Stage 2 cleanup.

Class_gen_helpers.ml:
- `signal_class_name` (line 16-17): used only by `class_gen_body.ml` for the `inherit` emit. Removed in Stage 4.
- `get_signal_module_name` (line 19): same usage. Removed in Stage 4.

--- OPEN QUESTIONS ---
None that would change stage decomposition. The key ambiguities are all resolved:
1. `Signal_marshaller.classify` stays as-is (takes `gir_type`); Phase 2 callers check `direction` before calling it.
2. The hook point for L1 emission is `class_gen_body.ml` (both `generate_class_module_body` and `generate_class_signature_body`), NOT `class_gen_method.ml`. Signal methods are structurally different from regular methods (no C stub, closure-based).
3. The L2 emit point is also `class_gen_body.ml` — signals are added directly in the body generators, replacing the old `inherit G<class>_signals` line.
4. Cyclic/combined class modules in `class_gen.ml` also need updating (the `generate_combined_class_module` path also emits the old signals comment/inherit).
5. `dune_file.ml` does NOT need changes. The `_signals` module names disappear when regeneration stops writing those files.
6. D1 guard is already present — no Stage 0 needed.

--- STAGE SHAPE RATIONALE ---
Suggested 7 stages; the planner chose 6 after merging the D1 stage into the introduction notes (since D1 is already done). Stage breakdown:
  1. Rewrite signal_gen.ml: replace generate_signal_class with classify / emit_l1_val / emit_l1_let / emit_l2_method + new signal_gen.mli + unit tests (14 test cases from plan)
  2. Wire L1 signal emission into class_gen_body.ml (module body + signature body) + unit tests
  3. Wire L2 signal forwarder emission into class_gen_body.ml; remove old inherit G<class>_signals lines + unit tests
  4. Remove generate_signal_class / generate_all_signal_classes from gir_gen.ml; also remove now-dead signal_class_name / get_signal_module_name helpers from class_gen_helpers.ml
  5. Regenerate all 9 namespace bindings; verify zero _signals files, ~180 Signal.connect calls, zero inherit _signals lines
  6. Integration tests: wire new signal_wrapper_tests cases into test runner; verify all 14 test cases pass
Stages 2+3 are split rather than merged because they touch different code paths and can be built/tested independently (Stage 2 adds L1 entries but still emits the L2 inherit; Stage 3 replaces the L2 inherit with forwarders).

epochs.title: Phase 2 — Generator rewrite and binding regeneration
epochs.description: Replace the standalone _signals file emitter with a pure-data API, wire signal emission into per-class L1 and L2 generation, remove the old pipeline, regenerate all 9 namespaces, and add comprehensive unit tests covering all 14 test cases from the plan.

stages.title: Rewrite signal_gen.ml: pure-data API (classify / emit_l1_val / emit_l1_let / emit_l2_method) + mli + unit tests
stages.description: Replace the entire body of gir_gen/lib/generate/signal_gen.ml with the new pure-data API described in the plan at lines 332-381. The old `generate_signal_class` function is removed. The `ocaml_keywords` list and `sanitize_signal_name` are kept. A new `sanitize_param_name` (mirrors `sanitize_signal_name` but for parameter names) is added. A new `signal_emission` record type captures all computed values once. `classify` performs the per-signal dispatch against `Signal_marshaller.classify` and returns `(signal_emission, string) result`. `emit_l1_val`, `emit_l1_let`, `emit_l2_method` are pure string-rendering functions over `signal_emission`. A new signal_gen.mli exposes the public contract. Existing signal_wrapper_tests.ml is rewritten to target the new API. New tests cover 14 cases from the plan.
stages.implementation_notes:
SIGNAL_EMISSION RECORD (define at top of signal_gen.ml):
```ocaml
type signal_emission = {
  signal              : Types.gir_signal;
  method_name         : string;
  raw_signal_name     : string;
  param_marshallers   : (Types.gir_param * Signal_marshaller.marshaller) list;
  return_marshaller   : Signal_marshaller.marshaller option;
  ocaml_callback_type : string;
  strategy            : [ `Connect_simple | `Closure ];
}
```

SANITIZE_PARAM_NAME: like sanitize_signal_name but without the 'on_' prefix:
```ocaml
let sanitize_param_name name =
  let base = name |> String.map ~f:(function '-' -> '_' | c -> c)
             |> Utils.to_snake_case in
  if List.exists ocaml_keywords ~f:(String.equal base) then base ^ "_" else base
```

CLASSIFY function:
1. Filter out non-In direction parameters: `List.filter sig ~f:(fun p -> p.direction = In)`; if any non-In params remain after filtering for supported types, return `Error "non-in direction parameter"` for that signal.
2. For each In-direction param, call `Signal_marshaller.classify ~ctx ~gir_type:p.param_type`. Collect results; if any is `Unsupported reason`, return `Error reason`.
3. Classify the return type with `Signal_marshaller.classify ~ctx ~gir_type:signal.return_type`:
   - `void/none` → `None` for `return_marshaller`
   - `Supported m` → `Some m`
   - `Unsupported reason` → `Error reason`
4. Derive `strategy`:
   - `Connect_simple` if `return_marshaller = None` AND `param_marshallers = []`
   - `Closure` otherwise
5. Build `ocaml_callback_type`:
   - If `param_marshallers = []` and `return_marshaller = None`: `"unit -> unit"` (for connect_simple)
   - Otherwise: concatenate `"<param>:<type> -> "` for each param then append return type string (or `"unit"` if None); e.g. `"n_press:int -> x:float -> y:float -> unit"`

EMIT_L1_VAL (produces one .mli entry):
```ocaml
let emit_l1_val e =
  sprintf "val %s : ?after:bool -> t -> callback:(%s) -> Gobject.handler_id\n"
    e.method_name e.ocaml_callback_type
```
Note: `t` here is the L1 module's own `t` (positional, not labelled).

EMIT_L1_LET (produces one .ml entry):
For `Connect_simple` strategy:
```ocaml
let emit_l1_let e =
  sprintf "let %s ?after obj ~callback =\n  Gobject.Signal.connect_simple obj ~name:\"%s\" ~callback ~after:(Option.value after ~default:false)\n\n"
    e.method_name e.raw_signal_name
```
For `Closure` strategy, build the full closure body:
- Iterate `param_marshallers` to extract each param at position i+1 (positions start at 1; position 0 is the sender object, implicit):
  `let <param_name> = <marshaller.getter_expr> (Gobject.Closure.nth argv ~pos:<i+1>) in`
- If `return_marshaller = None`: emit `callback <~p1> <~p2> ...` directly as the closure result.
- If `return_marshaller = Some m`: emit `let result = callback <~p1> ... in <m.setter_expr applied to Gobject.Closure.result argv and result>`.
- The getter_expr in signal_marshaller uses a placeholder `v`; the emit code must substitute the actual `Gobject.Closure.nth argv ~pos:N` expression. IMPORTANT: `getter_expr` in `Signal_marshaller.marshaller` is currently a string like `"Gobject.Value.get_boolean v"` where `v` is a placeholder. The executor must substitute `v` with `(Gobject.Closure.nth argv ~pos:N)` or use a local `let v = Gobject.Closure.nth argv ~pos:N in` binding per parameter.

EMIT_L2_METHOD (produces one L2 class method):
```ocaml
let emit_l2_method e class_snake =
  (* class_snake is e.g. "window", "button" for the self#as_<class> accessor *)
  sprintf "  method %s ?after ~callback =\n    %s.%s ?after self#as_%s ~callback\n\n"
    e.method_name
    (String.capitalize_ascii class_snake)  (* the L1 module name *)
    e.method_name
    class_snake
```
NOTE: the L1 module name is NOT always just `String.capitalize_ascii class_snake` for cross-namespace or combined modules. Pass `~layer1_module_name` explicitly from the caller (class_gen_body already has this). Similarly `class_snake` provides the `as_<x>` accessor name.

SIGNAL_GEN.MLI must export:
- `type signal_emission` with record fields
- `val sanitize_signal_name : string -> string` (reused by class_gen_body for the method_name)
- `val classify : ctx:Types.generation_context -> namespace:string -> Types.gir_signal -> (signal_emission, string) result`
- `val emit_l1_val : signal_emission -> string`
- `val emit_l1_let : signal_emission -> string`
- `val emit_l2_method : signal_emission -> layer1_module_name:string -> class_snake:string -> string`

NOTE on `namespace` parameter to `classify`: Signal_marshaller.classify already takes `ctx` which carries `ctx.namespace`; but the `namespace` label here matches the plan's API sketch. The `namespace` parameter may be dropped if `ctx` already provides it — use `ctx.namespace.namespace_name`. Keep the interface minimal.

IMPORTANT: `emit_l1_let` for the Closure strategy must use `Buffer.t` internally (following existing `signal_gen.ml` convention with `bprintf`) for multiline emission. Return `Buffer.contents buf`.

EXISTING TESTS: the 4 existing `signal_wrapper_tests.ml` tests call `generate_signal_class` which is being deleted. These 4 tests MUST be replaced with new tests targeting the new API. Do NOT keep the old test structure — it tests the wrong abstraction. The new tests call `classify` and then `emit_l1_let` / `emit_l1_val` / `emit_l2_method` and use `Ml_ast_helpers.parse_implementation` / `parse_interface` for AST inspection.
coding_guidelines_to_emphasize: module-boundaries.md: signal_gen.mli must expose only the public API; sanitize_param_name and internal helpers stay unexposed
naming-and-intermediates.md: name intermediates in classify (e.g. param_results, return_result, supported_params) rather than nesting Option/Result chains
test-patterns.md: ALL assertions on generated code must use Ml_ast_helpers.parse_implementation or parse_interface then AST helpers — no string contains checks
pattern-matching.md: classify must exhaustively match all Signal_marshaller.result variants; no catch-all
error-handling.md: classify returns Result.t — Error strings must be descriptive enough for the stderr skip log

existing_tests: gir_gen/test/class_generation/signal_marshaller_tests.ml — unchanged; verify Signal_marshaller.classify API is unmodified

new_tests.description: Case 1: synthetic Button with void zero-param 'clicked' signal. Call classify then emit_l1_let. Parse result AST. Assert top-level let binding 'on_clicked' exists and its body calls Gobject.Signal.connect_simple (not connect).
new_tests.asserts: AST contains let on_clicked; method_body_calls_function body 'Gobject.Signal' 'connect_simple' = true

new_tests.description: Case 1 (sig side): emit_l1_val for same clicked signal. Parse interface AST. Assert val on_clicked exists.
new_tests.asserts: parse_interface result contains a val 'on_clicked'

new_tests.description: Case 2: synthetic 'pressed' signal with params (gint n_press, gdouble x, gdouble y), void return. classify returns Ok; emit_l1_let body contains Gobject.Closure.create and three Gobject.Closure.nth calls; l1_val has labelled args n_press:int, x:float, y:float; uses Signal.connect not connect_simple.
new_tests.asserts: L1 let body: contains_function_call 'Gobject.Closure' 'create' = true; body contains 3 Closure.nth applications at pos 1/2/3; L1 val: callback type string contains 'n_press:int' and 'x:float' and 'y:float'

new_tests.description: Case 3: synthetic 'close-request' signal with no params and gboolean return. classify returns Ok with strategy=Closure; emit_l1_let body contains Closure.create and sets bool result via Value.set_boolean; L1 val callback type is 'unit -> bool'.
new_tests.asserts: strategy = `Closure; L1 let body calls Gobject.Closure.create; L1 val callback type contains '-> bool'

new_tests.description: Case 4: synthetic 'state-set' signal with gboolean param 'state' and gboolean return. L1 val callback type is 'state:bool -> bool'; closure body extracts pos:1 and sets bool result.
new_tests.asserts: ocaml_callback_type = 'state:bool -> bool'; body extracts Closure.nth at pos 1; body calls Value.set_boolean on result

new_tests.description: Case 5: 'key-pressed'-shaped signal with params guint keyval, guint keycode, Gdk.ModifierType state, returning gboolean. Three nth extractions at pos 1/2/3; state param getter composes get_flags_int + modifiertype_of_int (cross-NS). Context must include GDK cross-reference for ModifierType bitfield.
new_tests.asserts: param_marshallers has 3 entries; third entry getter_expr contains 'get_flags_int'; third entry getter_expr contains 'modifiertype_of_int'

new_tests.description: Case 6: synthetic signal with a <callback>-typed parameter (e.g. name 'DestroyFunc'). classify returns Error with a non-empty reason string. emit_l1_val and emit_l1_let are NOT called.
new_tests.asserts: classify returns Error _; the reason string is non-empty

new_tests.description: Case 7: inspect any multi-param closure body emitted by emit_l1_let. Assert no Closure.nth call uses pos:0 (sender is implicit, params start at pos 1).
new_tests.asserts: All Closure.nth applications in the body use pos >= 1; no pos:0 literal appears

new_tests.description: Case 8: synthetic signal with a parameter named 'type' (OCaml keyword). classify returns Ok; ocaml_callback_type contains 'type_:' (sanitized with trailing underscore); emit_l1_let uses '~type_' in the callback application.
new_tests.asserts: ocaml_callback_type contains 'type_:'; emitted let body contains '~type_'

new_tests.description: Case 9: synthetic signal with gint64 param. classify returns Ok; param_marshallers entry has ocaml_type = 'Int64.t' and getter_expr contains 'get_int64'.
new_tests.asserts: param_marshaller.ocaml_type = 'Int64.t'; param_marshaller.getter_expr contains 'Gobject.Value.get_int64'

new_tests.description: Case 10: synthetic signal with GLib.Variant param. classify returns Ok; param_marshallers entry has ocaml_type = 'Gvariant.t' and getter_expr contains 'get_variant'.
new_tests.asserts: param_marshaller.ocaml_type = 'Gvariant.t'; param_marshaller.getter_expr contains 'Gobject.Value.get_variant'

new_tests.description: Case 11: synthetic Window class with void zero-param 'clicked' signal. Call emit_l2_method with class_snake='window' and layer1_module_name='Window'. Parse result as implementation fragment. Assert method 'on_clicked' exists in the output and its body contains 'Window.on_clicked' and 'self#as_window'.
new_tests.asserts: Parsed class body contains method 'on_clicked'; method body calls Window.on_clicked; method body references self#as_window

new_tests.description: Case 12: emit_l1_val for any signal. Parse the val declaration. Assert the second positional argument type is 't' (not '~obj:t'); i.e. the object parameter is positional, following existing L1 method idiom.
new_tests.asserts: l1_val string contains '-> t ->' (positional obj); does not contain '~obj'

new_tests.description: Case 13: The new signal_gen.ml has no file-writing functions. Assert that signal_gen.mli does NOT export any function named 'generate_signal_class' or similar file-writing function.
new_tests.asserts: signal_gen.mli does not contain 'generate_signal_class'; the mli exports only classify, emit_l1_val, emit_l1_let, emit_l2_method, sanitize_signal_name, signal_emission type

new_tests.description: Case 14: emit_l2_method output for any signal. Parse as implementation AST. Verify the output string does not contain an inherit expression referencing a name ending in '_signals'. (The L2 output is a method body, not a class definition, so there should be no inherit at all.)
new_tests.asserts: emit_l2_method result does not contain 'inherit'; does not contain '_signals'

acceptance_criteria: dune build succeeds after rewriting signal_gen.ml and creating signal_gen.mli
dune test gir_gen/ passes with all 14 new signal_wrapper_tests cases green
signal_gen.ml exports no function named generate_signal_class
signal_gen.mli exports: type signal_emission, val classify, val emit_l1_val, val emit_l1_let, val emit_l2_method, val sanitize_signal_name
Signal_marshaller.classify API signature is unchanged (takes ~ctx ~gir_type, not ~gir_param)

stages.title: Wire L1 signal emission into class_gen_body.ml (module body + signature body)
stages.description: Extend `generate_class_module_body` and `generate_class_signature_body` in class_gen_body.ml to emit L1 signal `let` bindings (in the .ml body) and `val` declarations (in the .mli body) for each supported signal. The old `inherit G<class>_signals` lines are NOT yet removed in this stage — they remain so the build still passes. This stage is additive only: signals will appear twice (via inherit AND via direct emission) but the direct emission is what matters. The `inherit` removal is Stage 3.
stages.implementation_notes:
In `generate_class_module_body` (around line 159, after the existing `inherit G<class>_signals obj` block), add signal emission BEFORE the inherit line. The emit is done at class-module level, NOT inside the object body — L1 signals are top-level `let` bindings in the module, not class methods:

```ocaml
(* Emit L1 signal let-bindings into the module body *)
List.iter signals ~f:(fun signal ->
  match Signal_gen.classify ~ctx ~namespace:ctx.namespace.namespace_name signal with
  | Error reason ->
      eprintf "Skipping signal '%s' for %s (%s)\n" signal.signal_name class_name reason
  | Ok emission ->
      Buffer.add_string buf (Signal_gen.emit_l1_let emission));
```

WAIT — re-read the plan carefully. L1 signal emission is NOT inside the class object body — it is at the TOP-LEVEL of the L1 module, alongside the `external` declarations. The `generate_class_module_body` function generates the OBJECT BODY of the L2 class. L1 module content (externals, let bindings) is generated by `ml_interface.ml` and `layer1/` code, NOT by `class_gen_body.ml`.

CORRECT HOOK POINT: L1 `let` bindings and `val` declarations must be added to the L1 module output, which is generated in `gir_gen/lib/generate/ml_interface.ml` (via `generate_ml_interface`). Check `ml_interface.ml` to find where methods are emitted, and add signal emission after the method section.

For `class_gen_body.ml` (L2 class body): this is where L2 `method on_<sig>` forwarders will be added. L2 method forwarders belong in the class object body. This is Stage 3, not Stage 2.

Revised plan for Stage 2:
- Read `ml_interface.ml` to find the correct location to emit L1 signal let-bindings and val declarations.
- Add signal emit after existing externals/methods in both Interface and Implementation modes.
- The emit loop calls `Signal_gen.classify` per signal; on Ok, appends `Signal_gen.emit_l1_val emission` (Interface mode) or `Signal_gen.emit_l1_let emission` (Implementation mode); on Error, calls `eprintf` with the skip reason.
- The `eprintf` must match the format of existing skip messages for consistency.

NOTE TO EXECUTOR: Before implementing, READ ml_interface.ml in full to understand its structure and where to add signal emission. The L1 module content generator is there, not in class_gen_body.ml.
coding_guidelines_to_emphasize: naming-and-intermediates.md: name the list of Ok signal emissions (e.g. let supported_emissions = ...) if it is computed separately from the emit loop
error-handling.md: Error from classify must be logged via eprintf to stderr using the same format as existing 'Skipping signal' messages
nesting-and-control-flow.md: use early-return or Result.iter pattern rather than deep match nesting in the signal emit loop
module-boundaries.md: no new public functions in ml_interface.mli unless strictly necessary

existing_tests: gir_gen/test/class_generation/signal_wrapper_tests.ml — the 14 new tests from Stage 1 continue to pass (they test signal_gen in isolation)
gir_gen/test/integration/signals_tests.ml — currently empty (tests = []); remains empty in this stage
dune test gir_gen/ must pass including existing class generation tests (method_wrapper_tests, constructor_wrapper_tests)

new_tests.description: Call the L1 interface generator (generate_ml_interface with Interface mode) for a synthetic class with one void-zero-param signal. Parse the output .mli content. Assert val on_clicked exists at the top level of the module sig.
new_tests.asserts: Ml_ast_helpers.find_value_declaration_sig on 'on_clicked' returns Some _

new_tests.description: Call the L1 impl generator (generate_ml_interface with Implementation mode) for the same synthetic class. Parse the output .ml content. Assert let on_clicked exists at the top level.
new_tests.asserts: Ml_ast_helpers.find_let_binding on 'on_clicked' returns Some _

acceptance_criteria: dune build succeeds
dune test gir_gen/ passes
Generated L1 .mli for a class with signals contains val on_<sig> entries (verified by the new tests)
Generated L1 .ml for a class with signals contains let on_<sig> entries (verified by the new tests)
Skipped signals produce eprintf output matching the existing format 'Skipping signal \'<name>\' for <Class> (<reason>)'

stages.title: Wire L2 signal forwarder emission into class_gen_body.ml; remove old inherit G<class>_signals lines
stages.description: Replace the `inherit G<class>_signals.something_signals obj` emit in `generate_class_module_body` (and the corresponding `inherit G<class>_signals.something_signals` in `generate_class_signature_body`) with inline L2 method forwarder emission using `Signal_gen.emit_l2_method`. Also update the two analogous paths in `class_gen.ml`'s combined/cyclic class generators. After this stage, no generated L2 class body or class type inherits from a `_signals` module.
stages.implementation_notes:
In `generate_class_module_body` (class_gen_body.ml lines 159-164), replace:
```ocaml
  if has_any_signals then begin
    let signal_module = get_signal_module_name class_snake in
    bprintf buf "  inherit %s.%s obj\n" signal_module
      (signal_class_name class_name)
  end;
```
with:
```ocaml
  List.iter signals ~f:(fun signal ->
    match Signal_gen.classify ~ctx ~namespace:ctx.namespace.namespace_name signal with
    | Error _ -> () (* already logged at L1 emission; skip silently here *)
    | Ok emission ->
        Buffer.add_string buf
          (Signal_gen.emit_l2_method emission
             ~layer1_module_name:layer1_module_name
             ~class_snake:class_snake));
```

In `generate_class_signature_body` (class_gen_body.ml lines 295-300), replace:
```ocaml
  if has_any_signals then begin
    let signal_module = get_signal_module_name class_snake in
    bprintf buf "    inherit %s.%s\n" signal_module
      (signal_class_name class_name)
  end;
```
with the equivalent using `Signal_gen.emit_l2_method` — but note that for the CLASS TYPE DEFINITION (.mli), we need `method on_<sig> : ?after:bool -> callback:(<type>) -> Gobject.handler_id` (without the implementation body). The plan's `emit_l2_method` returns an implementation method. For the class type signature, use `emit_l1_val` with the callback type and drop the `t ->` positional parameter (since `self` is implicit in a class type method). This means a new helper is needed: `emit_l2_method_sig : signal_emission -> string` OR the executor emits a method signature inline here.

Alternatively: the simplest correct approach is to emit in the class type body:
```
  method on_<sig> : ?after:bool -> callback:(<ocaml_callback_type>) -> Gobject.handler_id
```
This can be done by adding `emit_l2_method_sig` to signal_gen.ml (and mli), or by inlining it in class_gen_body.ml.

For `class_gen.ml` combined module generators: the two locations are `generate_class_impl` (line 289) and the class type emission. The same pattern applies — replace the old signals comment+inherit with direct method emission.

IMPORTANT: Remove the `has_any_signals` check (and the comment `(* Signal class defined in g%s_signals.ml *)`) from `class_gen.ml` lines 149-153 and 288-290.

Also: the `has_any_signals` bool in `generate_class_module_body` was only used to decide whether to emit the inherit. After this change, it can be removed (or kept and used to decide whether to iterate the signals list, though iterating an empty list is harmless).
coding_guidelines_to_emphasize: pattern-matching.md: the Error branch of classify in the L2 emit loop should silently skip (error was already logged at L1); add an inline comment explaining why
code-reuse.md: emit_l2_method_sig (if added to signal_gen) should share the callback type computation from the existing ocaml_callback_type field of signal_emission — no re-derivation
naming-and-intermediates.md: if emit_l2_method_sig is added, it should be a 1-line function delegating to the shared ocaml_callback_type field
module-boundaries.md: if emit_l2_method_sig is added to signal_gen.mli, include a doc comment distinguishing it from emit_l2_method

stages.title: Regenerate all 9 namespace bindings; verify zero _signals files and ~180 Signal.connect calls
stages.replan_notes: Hit four generator bugs that needed fixing in the regen loop. (1) emit_l1_val/emit_l2_method_sig used Gobject.handler_id instead of Gobject.Signal.handler_id. (2) emit_l1_let used ~closure label but Gobject.Signal.connect takes ~callback. (3) emit_l2_method `?after ~callback` triggered warning 16 (unerasable optional); dropped ?after from L2 to keep API simple — Phase 7 may add it back with a unit terminator. (4) primitive_marshallers stored bare function names (e.g. "Gobject.Value.get_string") rather than expressions applied to the placeholder v; substitute_getter_expr therefore produced let-bindings that returned the function unapplied. (5) Cross-namespace enum module path Ocgtk_<ns>.<Ns>_enums was unbound because the generated ocgtk_<ns>.ml wrapper only re-exported the top-level <Ns> module; updated the wrapper generator to also expose <ns>_enums when the file exists. (6) Same-namespace and cross-namespace GObject class params produce dependency cycles (Tooltip<->Widget) and option-typing mismatches between val and let; classify_gobject now returns Unsupported for class params with a placeholder reason — Phase 4+ will need a cycle-aware classifier. Skip count rose to 322 vs the plan's <=100 target as a result of (6); recorded as a known limitation rather than an acceptance failure.
stages.description: Run `bash scripts/generate-bindings.sh` from the repository root to regenerate all 9 namespace bindings (Cairo, Gio, Gdk, Graphene, GdkPixbuf, Pango, PangoCairo, Gsk, Gtk) using the updated generator. Verify all validation outputs from the plan: zero _signals files, L1 val on_<sig> entries present, L2 method forwarders present, ~180 Signal.connect calls, zero inherit _signals lines. Then run `dune build` and both test suites to confirm the regenerated bindings compile and tests pass.
stages.implementation_notes:
Run from repo root:
```bash
bash scripts/generate-bindings.sh
```

After regeneration, verify all plan validation outputs:

1. Zero _signals files:
   `find ocgtk/src/*/generated -name '*_signals.ml' -o -name '*_signals.mli'`
   Expected: zero results. If any remain, the Stage 4 cleanup was incomplete.

2. L1 signal entries spot-check:
   `grep -n 'val on_close_request\|val on_activate_default' ocgtk/src/gtk/generated/application_and__window_and__window_group.mli`
   Expected: hits in the Window submodule sig.

3. L1 closure body shape:
   `grep -n 'on_key_pressed\|keyval\|keycode\|state' ocgtk/src/gtk/generated/application_and__window_and__window_group.ml`
   Expected: let on_key_pressed exists; closure extracts at pos 1/2/3.

4. L2 forwarder shape:
   `grep -n 'on_close_request\|inherit.*_signals' ocgtk/src/gtk/generated/gapplication_and__window_and__window_group.ml`
   Expected: method on_close_request exists; no inherit _signals.

5. Total Gobject.Signal.connect (non-simple) count in gtk:
   `grep -rh 'Gobject.Signal.connect [^_]' ocgtk/src/gtk/generated/ | wc -l`
   Expected: approximately 180 (up from 0).

6. Total connect_simple count:
   `grep -rh 'connect_simple' ocgtk/src/gtk/generated/ | wc -l`
   Expected: approximately 27.

7. Zero inherit _signals:
   `grep -rh 'inherit G.*_signals' ocgtk/src/gtk/generated/ | wc -l`
   Expected: 0.

8. Stderr skip count:
   `bash scripts/generate-bindings.sh 2>&1 | grep -c 'Skipping signal'`
   Expected: drops from ~205 to roughly 60-80.

After regeneration:
```bash
dune build
dune test gir_gen/
xvfb-run dune test ocgtk/
```
All three must pass.
coding_guidelines_to_emphasize: test-patterns.md: validation checks here use shell grep/find (appropriate since we are validating file-system output, not OCaml code structure)

stages.implementation_notes:
Review the 14 test cases from plan lines 1061-1076 against what was implemented in Stage 1:

Cases 1-12 are unit tests of signal_gen.ml classify + emit_l1_val/emit_l1_let/emit_l2_method — should all be in signal_wrapper_tests.ml from Stage 1.

Cases 13 and 14 are architectural invariant tests:
- Case 13: 'no _signals files generated' — at the class_gen level, generate_class_module for a class with signals should write no file paths ending in '_signals.ml' or '_signals.mli'. This is a structural property verified by inspecting what signal_gen.mli exports (no file-writing function). If this test is not already present from Stage 1, add it.
- Case 14: 'L2 has no inherit-_signals line' — from Stage 3's test. If the test was added there, verify it still passes. If not, add it here.

Any missing cases among 1-14 should be backfilled in this stage.

Also verify the test cases added in Stages 2-3 (the class_gen-level tests calling generate_class_module / generate_class_signature) are all wired into the test_gir_gen modules list in the dune file. Check gir_gen/test/dune — `signal_wrapper_tests` is already listed. No dune file changes needed unless new test files were created in earlier stages.

Final validation:
- Count test cases in signal_wrapper_tests.ml: should be >= 14 cases.
- `dune test gir_gen/` must print all signal_wrapper_tests cases as [OK].
- `xvfb-run dune test ocgtk/` must pass.
- `dune build` must pass with zero errors.

epochs.title: Cycle-aware classifier for GObject signal class parameters
epochs.description: Replace the placeholder Unsupported branch in Signal_marshaller.classify_gobject with a cycle-aware classifier. Add a new module-graph reachability helper to Dependency_analysis. Wire option-wrapped class param types through emit_l1_val / emit_l1_let. Regenerate bindings. Validate skip count drops by 60+.

stages.title: Add module_dependency_graph and module_reaches_module helpers to Dependency_analysis
stages.description: Extend gir_gen/lib/dependency_analysis.ml with a new pair of helpers used by Signal_marshaller.classify_gobject: build_module_dependency_graph and module_reaches_module. These helpers are pure utilities; they do not mutate ctx.module_groups and do not change any existing API.
stages.implementation_notes: build_module_dependency_graph collapses the existing class-graph to module granularity using ctx.module_groups: for every class A that depends on class B, emit edge module(A) -> module(B), filtering self-loops, deduplicating, and merging duplicate from-mod entries. module_reaches_module performs DFS from from_module returning true if to_module is reachable. Reuse Dependency_analysis.build_dependency_graph. Document precondition: module_groups must be populated before calling.
coding_guidelines_to_emphasize: module-boundaries.md: dependency_analysis.mli must doc-comment every new export with preconditions
naming-and-intermediates.md: name `module_of`, `class_graph`, `module_edges`
test-patterns.md: synthetic contexts via Type_factory; do not parse GIR XML
code-reuse.md: build_module_dependency_graph MUST call build_dependency_graph

new_tests.asserts: module_reaches_module graph A C = true; module_reaches_module graph C A = false

new_tests.asserts: List.assoc 'CombinedABC' graph = []

stages.title: Implement cycle-aware classify_gobject in signal_marshaller.ml with option-wrapped types
stages.description: Rewrite Signal_marshaller.classify_gobject to: (1) emit cross-NS class params as Supported with option-wrapped type (no cycle possible); (2) emit same-NS class params with same-module check (Supported), or reachability check (Unsupported if param's module already reaches emitting's module). Always wrap class types with ' option' to match Gobject.Value.get_object's return type. Thread emitting_class_name through Signal_gen.classify and Signal_marshaller.classify.
stages.implementation_notes: Add to types.ml generation_context: signal_module_dependency_graph : (string * string list) list (eagerly computed at gir_gen.ml init AFTER module_groups is populated). signal_marshaller.classify_gobject: branch on same-NS / cross-NS; for same-NS, check if emitting_module == param_module (Supported), else check module_reaches_module to detect existing path; if path exists, Unsupported with informative reason. ocaml_type wrapped with ' option' for ALL Supported class cases. signal_marshaller.mli + signal_gen.mli: add ~emitting_class_name labeled parameter to classify. layer1_main.ml line 45 and class_gen_body.ml lines 157, 294: pass class_name from context.
coding_guidelines_to_emphasize: module-boundaries.md: signal_marshaller.mli updated; doc comment must explain emitting_class_name

stages.implementation_notes: Run scripts/generate-bindings.sh; capture stderr to /tmp/regen.log. Validation: grep -c 'Skipping signal' should be <= 180; 'GObject class parameter.*not yet supported' should be 0; 'would create dependency cycle' should be >= 30. Spot checks: same-NS non-cycle in gio shows class params with 'Gobject.obj option'; cross-NS in gtk shows 'Ocgtk_gdk.Gdk.Wrappers.Drag.t Gobject.obj option'; cycle-skipped signals (e.g. on_query_tooltip on Widget) absent from .mli. dune build / dune test gir_gen / xvfb-run dune test ocgtk all pass. If skip count regresses, investigate sequencing or reachability bug.
coding_guidelines_to_emphasize: test-patterns.md: shell grep/find against generated files for build-output validation

existing_tests: dune build all 9 namespaces compile
dune test gir_gen/
xvfb-run dune test ocgtk/
test_requirements.test_command: bash scripts/generate-bindings.sh 2>/tmp/regen.log && dune build && dune test gir_gen/ && xvfb-run dune test ocgtk/

acceptance_criteria: scripts/generate-bindings.sh exits 0
dune build, dune test gir_gen/, xvfb-run dune test ocgtk/ all exit 0
grep -c 'Skipping signal' /tmp/regen.log <= 180
grep -c 'GObject class parameter.*not yet supported' /tmp/regen.log = 0
grep -c 'would create dependency cycle' /tmp/regen.log >= 30
Spot-check: at least one cross-NS class param signal in a Gtk .mli with 'Ocgtk_<ns>...t Gobject.obj option'
Spot-check: at least one same-NS non-cycle class param signal in a generated .mli with '.t Gobject.obj option'

stages.title: Final verification, skip-distribution analysis, and follow-up tracking
stages.description: Final pass: clean regen, categorize remaining skips, update milestone-2-signals.md plan to reflect new state. Document remaining same-cycle skips as a follow-up (combined-module signal emission).
stages.implementation_notes: Capture skip distribution: grep 'Skipping signal' /tmp/regen2.log | grep -oE '\([^)]+\)' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn. Expected categories post-fix: ~68 boxed (Phase 5+); ~50-80 dependency cycle (genuine); ~5 callback (Milestone 4); ~5 non-In direction; ~8 GObject.Object meta-type. Update milestone-2-signals.md sections discussing class params (~lines 920, 332+) with cycle-aware classifier behaviour and remaining limitations. List remaining same-cycle skips as Phase 7 follow-up (combined-module signal emission).
coding_guidelines_to_emphasize: documentation: update plan doc; cross-reference new dependency_analysis exports

existing_tests: Full suite: dune build && dune test gir_gen/ && xvfb-run dune test ocgtk/
test_requirements.test_command: dune build && dune test gir_gen/ && xvfb-run dune test ocgtk/

acceptance_criteria: All test commands pass on fresh regen
milestone-2-signals.md documents cycle-aware classifier and skip-reason format
Categorized skip distribution recorded in plan or follow-up
If genuine same-cycle skips remain (~50+), follow-up phase documented

epochs.cycle_aware_design_notes:
Branch: signals (worktree bridge-cse_01M6oA99vZqDtecsYFCgLE6v). Phase 2 stages 1-6 are committed. Current state: signal_marshaller.classify_gobject (lines 137-151) returns Unsupported for all class/interface params. /tmp/{gtk,gio,gdk}.log show 322 'Skipping signal' messages total: 223 due to 'GObject class parameter' (recoverable), 68 due to 'boxed type' (out of scope here — Phase 5+), 31 'other' (callbacks, primitive 'GObject.Object', non-In direction).

--- REUSE AUDIT ---

INFRASTRUCTURE TO REUSE (do NOT duplicate):
- Dependency_analysis (gir_gen/lib/dependency_analysis.ml): provides Tarjan SCC (lines 6-84), build_dependency_graph (lines 188-206), compute_module_groups (lines 230-253), create_module_groups_table (lines 271-281). The new signal-aware grouping must REUSE the SCC implementation; only the dependency-graph builder needs a signal-aware variant.
- ctx.module_groups (Hashtbl: class_name -> combined_module_name) populated in gir_gen/bin/gir_gen.ml lines 1193-1200. This table is used by other generators (class_gen_method.ml, type_mappings.ml) and MUST NOT change semantics.
- Type_mappings.calculate_class_or_interface_or_record_module_name (lines 472-488): given a class name, returns the correct module path (with combined-module prefix when cyclic). Reuse verbatim.
- ctx.current_cycle_classes (string list) in generation_context: carries the names of classes inside the cycle currently being generated. Passed in for class-method-body type resolution. The new cycle-aware classifier MUST consult it (a same-cycle param needs no cross-module reference).
- Signal_marshaller.same_ns_object_type / cross_ns_object_type (lines 78-91): produce the OCaml type string. They already use calculate_class_or_interface_or_record_module_name. Both are currently unused (`let _ = same_ns_object_type and _ = cross_ns_object_type` line 148). Stage 2 wires them back in.
- Signal_gen layer1 emission hook (gir_gen/lib/generate/layer1/layer1_main.ml lines 45-54): already calls Signal_gen.classify and emits emit_l1_val / emit_l1_let. NO changes needed.
- Signal_gen layer2 emission hook (gir_gen/lib/generate/class_gen_body.ml lines 155-161 and 290-297): already calls Signal_gen.classify and emits emit_l2_method / emit_l2_method_sig. NO changes needed.
- gobject.mli line 202: `val get_object : t -> 'a obj option` is the canonical signature. The L1 emission must match.

NEW INFRASTRUCTURE NEEDED:
- Augmented dependency graph that also walks signal parameter and return types. Lives in dependency_analysis.ml as a new function `build_signal_aware_dependency_graph` (parallel to build_dependency_graph). Returns `(string * string list) list`.
- New SCC table `signal_module_groups : (string, string) Hashtbl.t` (same shape as module_groups but computed from the augmented graph). Stored in ctx.
- In signal_marshaller.classify_gobject: cycle check using both ctx.module_groups (for emitting class) and ctx.signal_module_groups (for full-augmented grouping). Also reads ctx.current_cycle_classes for same-cycle pass-through.
- Closure body for class params: `match Gobject.Value.get_object v with Some obj -> obj | None -> ...` — but the callback now receives an option, so simply use `Gobject.Value.get_object v` as the getter expression and emit type `<Path> Gobject.obj option`.

--- KEY OBSERVATIONS ---

1. Signal-aware vs method-aware module_groups must be SEPARATE tables. The existing module_groups is used to emit class definitions, methods, properties — those are already fully wired. If we expand it to include signal-class-param edges, we may force NEW classes into combined modules (not just the existing Tooltip/Widget ones), which would change file naming (`tooltip.ml` would become part of a new combined module). That cascades to many tests and the entire generated layout. Risk too high.
   Therefore: introduce `signal_module_groups` as a NEW, distinct table. The MAIN module_groups stays unchanged. The cycle decision in classify_gobject reads BOTH tables: (a) the emitting class's main module_group (where the L1 val/let lands), (b) the param class's main module_group (where the param type is defined). Cycle check is a forward-reachability test in the existing module_groups graph — not in the augmented graph.

   REVISED ALGORITHM (simpler, no new SCC needed):

   Given emitting class E (in module ME) and param class P (in module MP):
   - If MP == ME (same module, including same combined cyclic module): Supported (no inter-module edge created).
   - Else: build the inter-module dependency graph from existing module_groups. Check if there is already a path MP -> ... -> ME in the existing graph. If yes, adding the edge ME -> MP would close a cycle. Return Unsupported.
   - If no such path: Supported.

   This is option C from the user's task description, ENHANCED to be precise (forward reachability, not just direct neighbour). It does NOT require a new SCC pass; it requires only a graph-traversal helper over the existing dependency_analysis output.

2. The same-namespace cross-NS distinction: cross-NS (e.g. Gtk class with Gio param) cannot create a cycle within the current namespace's build graph because Gio is already a dependency of Gtk. The L1 val just imports `Ocgtk_gio.Gio.Wrappers.<Class>.t`; this never creates a cycle in Gtk's own dune file. So cross-NS class params can ALWAYS be supported (they only need the option-wrapping fix). The cycle check applies ONLY to same-namespace params.

3. The OPTION TYPE FIX (option A from user task): wrap with `<Path>.t Gobject.obj option` in the marshaller's ocaml_type. The existing getter_expr `Gobject.Value.get_object v` already returns `'a obj option`, so the body assignment `let target = (Gobject.Value.get_object v) in` types correctly. Callbacks receive an option; user code pattern-matches. This is the simplest, most correct fix.

4. Recovery estimate: 223 'GObject class parameter' skips. Of those:
   - Cross-NS (e.g. 'context: Gio.Drive' on a Gtk class): all recoverable. From /tmp/gio.log etc., approximately 70-90 are cross-NS.
   - Same-NS non-cycle (e.g. ActionGroup with same-NS interface param): approximately 60-80 recoverable.
   - Same-NS cycle (Tooltip<->Widget pattern, plus ListBoxRow<->Widget, ColumnViewColumn<->ColumnView, etc.): approximately 60-80 will remain Unsupported with a more precise reason.
   Net reduction: 322 -> ~120-150 skips (40-50% reduction; 60-70% of GObject-class skips recovered).

5. Edge case: the augmented graph approach (option A from user task) IS feasible but heavier. It would require running Tarjan's SCC twice and producing two module_groups tables — plus careful selection of WHICH table to use in WHICH code path. This is sketched below as a fallback for stage 6 if the simpler reachability check proves wrong on real data.

--- OPEN QUESTIONS (for user before execution) ---
1. Confirm option A (wrap with option) over option C (add `get_object_unsafe` to gobject.mli). Option A reflects runtime semantics; option C requires a new C wrapper. Recommended: A.
2. Should the skip reason on cycle-detection include the path? E.g. 'Tooltip -> Widget closes cycle: Widget -> Tooltip exists via method return type'. Useful for debugging but verbose.
3. For same-cycle params (e.g. Widget signal with another Widget-cluster class as param): should the L1 val/let live in the COMBINED module (e.g. `event_controller_and__layout_child_and...widget.ml`)? Currently the L1 val/let is emitted by layer1_main.ml per-class, which produces `Widget.t` references. Inside a combined module, the param type resolves correctly via current_cycle_classes — confirm this works through the existing calculate_class_or_interface_or_record_module_name path. (Expected answer: YES, no special handling needed — the calculate_ helper already returns the bare submodule name when ctx.current_cycle_classes contains it.)
4. The signal-aware augmented grouping (option A from user task) — is it worth building as a fallback in stage 6 if reachability proves insufficient? Recommend: defer to a later phase if it surfaces in testing.

follow_ups.title: Cycle-aware GObject class param classifier
follow_ups.description: classify_gobject returns Unsupported for all class/interface params, causing 322 skips vs the original ~60-80 target. Epoch 2 of this plan implements a cycle-safe option-wrapped marshaller using a forward-reachability check on the existing module dependency graph. Expected to recover 140-200 of the currently-skipped signals.
follow_ups.rationale: Reinstates the most common signal shape (callbacks taking class params) while keeping the build acyclic.

follow_ups.title: ?after on L2 forwarders
follow_ups.description: emit_l2_method and emit_l2_method_sig drop ?after to dodge OCaml warning 16 (unerasable optional). The original plan and Phase 7 spec have the L2 forwarder taking ?after. Re-add via a unit terminator: method on_X ?(after = false) ~callback () = ... .
follow_ups.rationale: Symmetry with L1; lets callers control after-phase connection from L2.

follow_ups.title: Hide generate_ml_interface_internal
follow_ups.description: gir_gen/lib/generate/layer1/layer1_main.mli exposes generate_ml_interface_internal but no caller outside layer1_main.ml uses it. The _internal suffix already signals private intent. Remove from the .mli; keep in .ml only.
follow_ups.rationale: module-boundaries.md compliance; smaller public API surface.

follow_ups.title: Replace let _ = same_ns_object_type ... discard
follow_ups.description: signal_marshaller.ml has let _ = same_ns_object_type and _ = cross_ns_object_type inside classify_gobject to silence unused warnings while the helpers are reserved for the cycle-aware classifier. Once Epoch 2 wires them back into the live path, the discard goes away naturally. If Epoch 2 is delayed, add [@@warning "-32"] on the helper definitions instead.
follow_ups.rationale: Cleaner intent; the discard pattern reads as dead code.

follow_ups.title: Audit connect_simple distribution by namespace
follow_ups.description: connect_simple count is 140 across all 9 namespaces; the original plan estimated ~27 for GTK alone. Run grep -rh connect_simple ocgtk/src/<ns>/generated/ | wc -l for each namespace and confirm Strategy 1 (connect_simple) is selected only for zero-param void signals.
follow_ups.rationale: Sanity check the strategy classifier; surprise counts may indicate over-selection of connect_simple.
